message.content: {"top_matches":[{"player":"CHICKEN","probability":0.6},{"player":"RABBIT","probability":0.3},{"player":"CHICK","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["CHICKEN and RABBIT contributed in every round (consistent full cooperation), matching a steady, rule-following, civic-minded player (church attendance, patriotism).","CHICK shows frequent cooperation (4/6) and regularity (cooperates most rounds) so is a plausible secondary match for a politically engaged but occasionally strategic contributor.","Choices favor stable, predictable contributors over intermittently defecting or deceptive players (e.g., PARROT said 'All in' but defected), aligning with conservative, community-oriented values."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only six rounds of binary choices — limited behavioral signal to infer deep personality or political motives.","Multiple motivations (altruism, strategy, signaling) can produce identical contribution patterns; observable actions do not reveal private reasoning.","Demographic and political identity correlations with game behavior are probabilistic, not deterministic; assignment is therefore tentative."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"RABBIT","probability":0.5},{"player":"MOOSE","probability":0.3},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.2}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistent cooperation (gave 20 most rounds) combined with explicit statements about punishing noncontributors — matches an older, community- and norm-focused conservative who enforces rules.","Polite, community-oriented messaging (urging everyone to contribute) and occasional targeted punishment of defectors aligns with churchgoing, order-valuing personality.","Steady, declarative claims of contributing every time and occasional punishment indicate a no-nonsense, rule-abiding approach consistent with a conservative who values reciprocity and order."],"uncertainty_notes":["Inferred motives from short chat messages and punishment choices — actual private motivations (e.g., spite vs. norm enforcement) are not observable.","Several players show overlapping cooperative and punitive behavior (Duck, Parrot, Sloth, Chicken), so distinguishing fine-grained personality alignment is uncertain.","Interface/reporting/display inconsistencies mentioned by players (conflicting contribution views) could have affected behavior and punishment, complicating attribution of intent."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.82},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.10},{"player":"DUCK","probability":0.08}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH always contributes the full 20 each round — fits a stable, duty- and community-oriented profile (church attendance, strong norms, patriotism) and someone who values consistent public contribution.","GORILLA shows moderate contributions early, a sudden drop to 0 in round 5, then a full contribution in round 6 — suggests conditional/strategic behavior and occasional opportunism, less consistent with a norms-driven, steady contributor.","DUCK ramps up then defects in the final round — a classic endgame free-ride pattern that signals strategic opportunism rather than steady normative cooperation expected from a church-attending, patriotic older conservative."],"uncertainty_notes":["No messages or motivations are shown, so inferred motives (altruism, strategy, reciprocity) are speculative.","Behavior in a lab game may not map cleanly to political or cultural identity; individual differences (risk tolerance, understanding of payoff structure) could alter choices.","Sample is small (6 rounds) and players may be experimenting or responding to unobserved factors; assignment of probabilities reflects plausibility, not certainty."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.7},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.3}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH mostly contributed the full endowment (20) for four rounds, then withheld entirely in round 5 and partially in round 6 — consistent with a generally cooperative, norm-supporting player who reacts to perceived free-riding by withholding contribution rather than paying to punish directly.","My profile (older, church-attending, community- and norm-oriented conservative) suggests I would generally support the public good and uphold group norms, but I would also be willing to withhold cooperation when others under-contribute — matching SLOTH’s pattern of conditional cooperation and signaling instead of frequent costly punishment.","GORILLA’s constant full contributions align with unconditional high cooperativeness, which is plausible but slightly less consistent with a conservative, norm-enforcing but cautious player (who might respond to norm violations rather than remain fully unconditional)."],"uncertainty_notes":["Mapping political/demographic traits to specific in-game choices is imprecise; people with similar profiles can behave differently in strategic settings.","We observe a small number of rounds and only three players’ actions; motives (e.g., misunderstanding, testing, mistakes) cannot be determined from contributions alone.","One observed player (DUCK) used a punishment action oddly (punishing an always-cooperative player), which could indicate noise or idiosyncratic preferences not captured by demographic/political cues."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"CHICK","probability":0.60},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.30},{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["CHICK consistently contributed the maximum, posted moralizing messages about contributing ('contributing max every round makes the most for everyone'; 'leechers get nothing'), and repeatedly rewarded contributors — matching a norm-enforcing, community-oriented personality (consistent with church attendance and valuing civic duty).","GORILLA also consistently contributed, frequently rewarded contributors, and publicly admonished free-riders — matching a pragmatic, enforcement-oriented conservative who emphasizes rules and mutual benefit.","SLOTH occasionally admonished others, pushed contributors to act, and both rewarded and contributed regularly; this fits an older, blunt, no-nonsense communicator but less consistently prosocial than CHICK and GORILLA."],"uncertainty_notes":["Player identities (age, gender, ethnicity, political views) are not observable in the transcript; matches are based only on in-game messages, contribution patterns, and reward behavior.","Behavioral similarity to the provided personal profile (71‑year‑old, Hispanic, conservative, churchgoing, patriotic) is inferred indirectly from norm-enforcing and prosocial actions — this is speculative and not definitive.","Some players show mixed or sparse signals (e.g., large one-off rewards from SNAKE) that could also map to aspects of the profile; limited data prevents high-confidence assignment."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.60},{"player":"CHICK","probability":0.25},{"player":"SNAKE","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistent contribution of 20 coins across rounds (signals rule-following and commitment to the group public good).","Active chat encouraging others to contribute and using light humor — fits an older, sociable player who tries to maintain cohesion.","Uses mild punishments at times but mostly supportive — suggests a norm-enforcing yet community-minded temperament (matches someone who values order, tradition, and church/community).","CHICK consistently rewards many players and repeatedly contributes 20, indicating strong prosocial and charitable behavior consistent with church attendance and communal values.","SNAKE consistently advocates contributing, repeatedly contributes 20, and enforces norms via frequent punishments — aligns with a politically engaged player who strongly values compliance and is willing to sanction free-riders."],"uncertainty_notes":["No direct demographic or political signals in-game (age, ethnicity, ideology are not observable), so mapping from player actions to the provided personal profile is inferential.","Multiple players display overlapping behaviors (cooperation, encouragement, punishment). Distinguishing between an older conservative’s likely subtleties (mild enforcement, community encouragement) and a younger/stricter enforcer is uncertain.","In-group dynamics and strategic considerations (e.g., punishing a long-term defector) may drive behavior that is not directly tied to political ideology or personal values."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.7},{"player":"DOG","probability":0.2},{"player":"DUCK","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistent cooperation from round 1, persuasive and factual messaging (“If everyone contributed, we'd all make more.” and doing payoff math) matches an older, civically minded, policy-interested person who values collective benefit and clear reasoning.","Quiet, steady cooperation (DOG) fits a reserved, duty-oriented participant who contributes without fanfare—plausible for an older churchgoing conservative who prefers action over loud chat.","DUCK’s pragmatic concerns about time/payment and eventual switch to full cooperation align somewhat with a practical, community-minded older adult who may ask logistical questions before fully engaging."],"uncertainty_notes":["No direct political or demographic signals in the chat; mapping from observed game behavior to personal demographics/ideology is inherently uncertain.","Several players behaved cooperatively; distinguishing between mature civic duty and younger pro-social play is ambiguous.","Emotional tone and slang used by some players (PARROT, SLOTH) strongly suggest different age/personality, but absence of explicit self-identification limits confidence."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.55},{"player":"DOG","probability":0.30},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH acts like a visible coordinator and norm-enforcer: sends chat urging everyone to contribute, repeatedly punishes defectors (large and frequent punishments) — consistent with an older, politically engaged, patriotic person who pushes for group cooperation and enforces rules.","DOG also enforces cooperation (punishes many free-riders, punishes when they contributed while others didn’t) and occasionally alternates contributions — fits a rule-following but pragmatic/occasional-defector profile that matches a ‘weak’ partisan who cares about outcomes but sometimes acts strategically.","GORILLA frequently contributes and communicates briefly but is less punitive; this fits a cooperative, less confrontational stance that a cautious, churchgoing conservative might display if not taking on an enforcement role."],"uncertainty_notes":["Inferences map in-game punishment/contribution behavior to real-world personality traits, which is inherently uncertain.","Players’ private motives, risk preferences, and whether they are signaling political identity cannot be observed directly from the actions shown.","The user’s described conservatism and social values can be expressed in multiple game strategies (leader, enforcer, or steady contributor), so match probabilities reflect ambiguity."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SNAKE","probability":0.6},{"player":"DOG","probability":0.3},{"player":"CHICKEN","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["SNAKE: Repeatedly argued for maximum contribution, provided concrete payoff calculations, and threatened/punished defectors—matches a normative, rule-supporting, politically engaged personality that enforces group norms.","DOG: Frequently urged punishment, punished free-riders early, and pushed for max contributions—fits a disciplinarian, pro-norm actor who is willing to use costly enforcement.","CHICKEN: Consistently advised against punishment as wasteful and emphasized personal payoff—aligns with a pragmatic, cautious approach consistent with a ‘weak’ partisan who prefers cooperation without costly enforcement."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavioral signals are limited to in-game chat and costly actions; real-world political identity (age, ethnicity, religion) may not map cleanly to these choices.","Multiple players display overlapping behaviors (encouraging max contributions but differing on punishment), so assignment is probabilistic.","Private motives and unobserved off-chat behavior could differ; final-round choices (e.g., leniency or harsh punishment) introduce ambiguity in matching."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"PARROT","probability":0.5},{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.3},{"player":"CHICKEN","probability":0.2}],"behavioral_basis":["PARROT initiated group coordination to contribute everything in the last five rounds and repeatedly encouraged/praised the group — fits a politically engaged person who would organize and promote collective action.","SLOTH contributed 20 in most rounds and promptly agreed to the proposed plan with minimal chat — matches a steady, reliable, norm-following individual (churchgoing, patriotic, older).","CHICKEN frequently rewarded many players and gave positive social feedback (\"good job everyone\") — consistent with a communal, encouraging personality who reinforces cooperation, matching church/community values."],"uncertainty_notes":["Inferring real-world demographics/ideology from game actions is uncertain; similar behaviors can reflect strategy rather than personality.","Players may adapt to others’ actions or promises (e.g., the last-five-rounds pledge), so behavior could be situational.","Chat and reward signals are limited; absence of explicit political content means mapping to the provided political profile is approximate."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"DUCK","probability":0.55},{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.35},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["High, consistent contributions match my preference for cooperation and communal/faith-based values (many rounds show 20).","Willingness to enforce norms but not recklessly: DUCK punishes free-riders moderately and also rewards cooperators, matching a conservative, churchgoing tendency to uphold norms while being measured.","SLOTH is ideologically similar in strong pro-cooperation messaging and norm enforcement (explicit messages about giving all and heavy punishment), which resonates with my moralizing streak but is more extreme than I’d likely act.","GORILLA’s consistent full contributions and quiet agreement fit my cooperative, rule-following side but the lack of social messaging makes it a weaker match to my talkative, politically interested personality."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavior in an experimental PGG may not map directly onto real-world political identity or age/ethnicity.","Punishment behavior is costly and may reflect momentary strategy, emotion, or misunderstanding rather than stable personality traits.","Chat frequency and tone provide clues but are limited; some players may mask true preferences due to game incentives."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.7},{"player":"COW","probability":0.2},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH contributed the full 20 every round (consistent cooperative behavior) and frequently rewarded others, often with targeted larger rewards — matches a norm‑upholding, publicly‑spirited, consistent contributor (fits an older, churchgoing, patriotic profile).","COW also contributed almost always and gave widespread small positive rewards to many players each round — aligns with steady, community‑oriented generosity typical of a religious, civic‑minded person.","GORILLA mostly contributed but had occasional zero contributions and gave selective rewards — plausible as a ‘weak’ partisan who is generally cooperative but sometimes withholds cooperation."],"uncertainty_notes":["Mapping political/demographic profile onto laboratory PGG behavior is imprecise; multiple players show overlapping cooperative/rewarding patterns.","Rewards alone don't reveal motive (indiscriminate generosity vs. norm enforcement), so assignment reflects best available behavioral resemblance, not certainty about internal motives.","Some players (e.g., PARROT, OWL, SNAKE) show mixed patterns that could also plausibly match parts of the persona under different assumptions."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.6},{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.3},{"player":"CHICK","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["GORILLA: Almost always contributed, issued many small rewards to cooperators and few punishments after a lone defection—matches a community-minded, norm-supporting person who prefers positive reinforcement (consistent contributor, active but not vindictive).","SLOTH: Always contributed and frequently used small punishments against defectors—matches a traditional, rule-enforcing, older conservative who upholds norms and sanctions free-riders.","CHICK: Actively rewarded many players and sometimes punished, but had a couple of early defections—matches a socially engaged churchgoer who is overall cooperative but occasionally deviates (fits ‘weak’ partisan behavior)."],"uncertainty_notes":["Inferring stable personal traits from a lab public-goods game is speculative; multiple motives (retaliation, strategic signaling, error) can produce similar actions.","Several players show mixed punish/reward patterns that could plausibly map to the described persona; the ranking reflects my best-guess alignment rather than a definitive match.","Behavioral expressions of political/conservative identity in this game are ambiguous (e.g., both rewarding cooperators and punishing defectors are plausible conservative-leaning responses), so probabilities are approximate."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"CHICKEN","probability":0.6},{"player":"RABBIT","probability":0.25},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistent pro-cooperation and leadership: CHICKEN repeatedly urged full contributions early, chided defectors as 'greedy', apologized when making a mistake—matches a conscientious, duty-oriented player (church-going, community-minded).","Pragmatic, steady contributor: RABBIT declared being 'max since the start', made a payoff estimate and encouraged others—matches a politically engaged, practical thinker concerned with outcomes.  (Though Rabbit defected occasionally, the overall pattern is instrumental cooperation.)","Supportive and steady: GORILLA quickly agreed, encouraged 'all in', and celebrated group success—fits a reliable, socially affirming persona likely to back collective efforts.)"],"uncertainty_notes":["No player mentions politics, age, gender, or explicit cultural markers, so mapping is based only on in-game cooperation style and chat tone.","Binary contribution choices and occasional misclicks limit ability to infer deeper preferences or true temperament.","Some players display mixed behavior (e.g., occasional defections), so assignment is probabilistic rather than certain."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"MOOSE","probability":0.6},{"player":"PARROT","probability":0.25},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["MOOSE frequently advocates norm enforcement, punishes free riders repeatedly, leads conversation and organizes group behavior — consistent with an older, rule-oriented, civically engaged person who values social order.","PARROT emphasizes maximizing group payoff and applies targeted punishments early to enforce cooperation, matching a pragmatic, outcome-focused style aligned with civic-minded political interest.","GORILLA is quiet, cooperative, and pragmatic in chat (occasional concise agreement and practical observations), matching a modest, low-key conservative (weak partisan) who prefers not to escalate conflict."],"uncertainty_notes":["Chat contains little or no explicit political content, age, race, gender, and flag-related preferences are not expressed by players, so matching is based on social-norm enforcement and conversational tone rather than direct ideological signals.","Players may be role-playing or not reflecting real-world personalities; behaviors in a short game can differ from long-term political/social preferences.","Some players show mixed behaviors (e.g., occasional joking or inconsistent geographic claims), increasing uncertainty in the match."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"OWL","probability":0.5},{"player":"DOG","probability":0.3},{"player":"COW","probability":0.2}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistent full contributions (OWL and DOG gave 20 every round) map onto an older, civic-minded, religious, and patriotic personality — a strong inclination to support collective goods.","COW contributed in 9/10 rounds, indicating high but slightly conditional cooperation, which fits a ‘weak Republican’ conservative who is generally community-oriented but may occasionally act in self-interest.","The user’s age, church attendance, strong interest in politics, and pronounced patriotism (positive affect toward the American flag) align with reliably cooperative behavior in a public-goods setting — these players best match that pattern."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavior in an anonymous PGG is an imperfect proxy for political identity or personal values; cooperation can be driven by many factors (risk preferences, expectations about others, experiment framing).","Multiple players show identical or very similar contribution patterns (several players contributed every round), so distinguishing among them by personality is partly speculative.","Names/avatars provide no real-life demographic cues; matching is based solely on behavioral similarity to the described personality, not on any direct evidence linking labels to demographics."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.65},{"player":"MONKEY","probability":0.2},{"player":"CROCODILE","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistent contributor (GORILLA contributed 20 in every round) matching a norm‑following, community‑oriented profile (church attendance, patriotic).","Frequent, targeted but moderate punishment by GORILLA (mostly single units aimed at defectors) fits a measured norm‑enforcer rather than an extremely aggressive punishers.","MONKEY and CROCODILE are secondary matches: both mostly contributed and punished defectors, with MONKEY using smaller, occasional punishments (more measured) and CROCODILE using more frequent/stronger punishments (more forceful norm enforcement)."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavioral mapping from game actions to personal traits is imprecise—many motives (strategic, retaliatory, experimental) can produce similar actions.","We only observe aggregate choices (contribute vs withdraw, and punishment amounts) without private beliefs or context about why someone punished.","Political/religious demographic cues don’t deterministically predict in‑game behavior; the assignment is an informed guess based on cooperation and punishment patterns."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.65},{"player":"CHICKEN","probability":0.25},{"player":"DUCK","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH explicitly identified as US and was largely quiet in chat—matches a 71-year-old conservative Hispanic man who might not chat much but notes nationality/patriotism.","SLOTH initially withheld contribution then switched to full cooperation after prompts, consistent with cautiousness and deference to group norms (churchgoing/community-oriented values).","CHICKEN contributed fully from the start and made a minimal supportive comment, consistent with duty-driven cooperation and low chat activity.","DUCK was very chatty and clearly from the UK; their sociability and nationality make them a less likely match despite strong pro-cooperation behavior."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavioral signals are limited and indirect; chat content and contribution choices can reflect momentary strategy rather than stable identity or political views.","No one explicitly expressed the specific cultural/political markers (Hispanic background, church attendance, flag preference), so matching relies on loose inferences (e.g., nationality mention, verbosity, cooperation style).","Probabilities reflect relative plausibility, not certainty; several players could plausibly map to aspects of the provided profile."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.7},{"player":"CHICKEN","probability":0.2},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH shows steady, moderately high contributions across almost all rounds (15–18). This fits an older, community-oriented, church-attending person who values stability and civic duty without extreme risk-taking.","CHICKEN consistently gives the maximum (mostly 20), indicating very strong pro-social/patriotic behavior; this aligns with strong visible patriotism (finding the flag very pleasing) but is somewhat more extreme than the user's self-described 'weak Republican' stance.","GORILLA is highly active in norm enforcement via frequent large rewards to others (and variable personal contributions). This maps to a politically engaged person who actively rewards/praises perceived cooperators, reflecting high political interest, but GORILLA's variable contributions make them a less close match than SLOTH or CHICKEN."],"uncertainty_notes":["Mapping political ideology, age, and cultural identity to choices in an abstract economic game is imprecise—many behavioral drivers (risk preferences, strategic incentives, social preferences) are not directly observed.","We only observe actions in this specific 29-round PGG with four players; absence of chat or explicit motives limits inference about underlying motives (patriotism, religiosity, or partisanship).","Some players display mixed signals (e.g., GORILLA rewards heavily but contributes variably); assigning probabilities reflects best-fit judgment rather than certainty."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.65},{"player":"DUCK","probability":0.25},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["High and sustained contributions by SLOTH fit a church-attending, patriotic older conservative who is willing to support the group and public goods.","SLOTH shows some adjustment (not blindly idealistic), which matches a weak Republican who is cooperative but pragmatic.","DUCK’s pattern (strong early cooperation then collapse to zero) looks like initial idealism that becomes discouraged — plausible but less consistent with steady civic-mindedness tied to patriotism and church.","GORILLA’s consistently low contributions suggest self-interested or free-riding behavior, which is least consistent with a socially engaged, churchgoing, patriotic elder."],"uncertainty_notes":["Mapping political/demographic traits to lab-game behavior is imprecise — individuals within groups vary widely.","Observed actions could reflect learning, experimentation, or responses to others that don’t directly reveal stable personal values.","Single odd rounds (e.g., SLOTH’s round-15 zero) could be anomalies or noise rather than true preference signals."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.75},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.20},{"player":"DUCK","probability":0.05}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH contributed 20 almost every round (two small deviations), consistent with a normative, duty-driven player — aligns with an older, church-attending, patriotic conservative who would prioritize contributing to the group.","GORILLA often contributed 20 but had multiple moderate (10) contributions — matches a pragmatic, weak-Republican stance: generally cooperative but occasionally conserving private resources.","DUCK showed low and highly variable contributions (many near 0–10, one 20) which is inconsistent with my described values of strong interest in civic duty, patriotism, and church/norm adherence."],"uncertainty_notes":["No punishments were ever used, so enforcement/retaliation tendencies (which my profile might incline toward) are unobserved and add uncertainty to matching.","The relation between political conservatism and PGG contributions is ambiguous in literature (could predict either higher norm-conformist cooperation or more emphasis on private holdings), so assignment is probabilistic.","Age, culture, and individual temperament can shift behavior; the observed pattern fits but is not a perfect one-to-one mapping."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.6},{"player":"DUCK","probability":0.3},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH shows consistently high contributions (many 20s, especially in later rounds) and repeatedly rewards other cooperators — matches a steady, community-minded player who values duty and reciprocity (consistent with church attendance and patriotism).","DUCK is also a reliable contributor with frequent mid-to-high contributions and occasional rewarding of cooperators — fits a cooperative, engaged player though slightly less consistently reciprocal than SLOTH.","GORILLA frequently gives rewards to many players (a norm-enforcer/active reciprocator) and often contributes highly, but has several low/zero contribution rounds — plausible for someone politically engaged who sometimes responds strongly (positive or negative) but is less consistently steady than SLOTH or DUCK."],"uncertainty_notes":["Mapping demographic/political traits to in-game contributions and reward behavior is inferential and noisy; multiple behavioral motives can produce similar actions.","Players’ strategies change over time in the data (occasional defections by otherwise cooperative players), so a match reflects overall tendency, not exact identity.","Rewards can reflect many motives (reciprocity, signaling, retaliation); interpreting them as aligned with political/cultural traits is uncertain."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.6},{"player":"CHICKEN","probability":0.25},{"player":"PARROT","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistently contributed the full 20 most rounds (values group cooperation and rule-following).","GORILLA used chat to call out a defector and encourage cooperation (aligns with someone who publicly upholds norms).","CHICKEN and PARROT were steady, low-profile cooperators—fits a conventional, community-oriented demeanor."],"uncertainty_notes":["Personality inferences drawn only from contribution and chat behavior; political/ demographic traits are not observable in the game.","Limited punishment/reward actions reduce ability to judge enforcement preferences.","Some actions (misclicks, strategic play) could reflect mistakes or strategy rather than stable personality."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.7},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.25},{"player":"CHICKEN","probability":0.05}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH always contributed (consistent cooperation) and punished free-riders relatively strongly (rounds 1 and 3) — matches a norm-enforcing, duty-oriented stance consistent with a church-attending, conservative outlook.","GORILLA also always cooperated but favored small rewards for cooperators and light punishments — matches a pro-social, reward-oriented variant of cooperation (still plausible for someone politically engaged but less punitive).","CHICKEN cooperated mostly but defected once and was punished — plausible as a minor mismatch (occasional deviation from strict norm enforcement)."],"uncertainty_notes":["Actions in the game reflect strategic choices under anonymity and may not map cleanly to political or cultural identity.","We cannot observe motivations, emotions, or off-game preferences (e.g., patriotism) that were described in the profile.","Only 6 rounds and 4 players — limited data; other players could be similar in unseen ways."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"RABBIT","probability":0.60},{"player":"DOG","probability":0.25},{"player":"DUCK","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["RABBIT contributes the maximum (20) every round — matches a steady, group‑oriented, patriotic and churchgoing profile that values community/public goods.","DOG contributes 20 in almost all rounds (only 15 on round 1) — consistent high cooperation, plausible for an older conservative with strong in‑group loyalty.","DUCK quickly shifts to near‑constant maximum contributions from round 4 onward — represents a reliably cooperative strategy that a civically engaged, politically interested person might adopt."],"uncertainty_notes":["Player identity is inferred solely from contribution patterns; many behaviors (strategy, reciprocity, experimentation) can produce similar contribution traces.","The participant described is a ‘weak’ Republican — this could lead to either steady high contributions (community/patriotic motives) or more self‑interested/conditional play; the data do not uniquely identify motives.","Other observed players also show high contributions at times (e.g., CROCODILE, COW, SLOTH), so assignment is probabilistic rather than certain."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.65},{"player":"PARROT","probability":0.2},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistent full contributions from early rounds (reliable, cooperative behavior likely for an older, community-oriented player).","SLOTH’s brief, practical chat messages (\"agree\", \"keep going\", \"it might end if someone leaves\") match a reserved, pragmatic communication style expected from an older, steady participant.","PARROT’s minimal but affirmative participation (mostly silent, consistently contributed 20) fits a less-chatty, steady player who nonetheless supports group cooperation.","GORILLA showed practical concerns about payout and stayed cooperative after initial caution, matching some aspects of an engaged, politically aware person who checks rules and remains engaged."],"uncertainty_notes":["No player referenced politics, age, religion, or explicit patriotic sentiment, so mapping is based only on sparse chat style and contribution patterns.","Several players switched from lower to full contributions after round 1, so initial-round behavior may reflect misunderstanding or interface issues rather than stable preferences.","Player labels and short messages limit inference; multiple players could plausibly match the profile given the limited signals."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"SLOTH","probability":0.62},{"player":"DOG","probability":0.23},{"player":"GORILLA","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["SLOTH contributed the maximum (20) in both rounds, matching a profile of strong civic/pro-social commitment consistent with a patriotic, church-attending 71-year-old who values community norms.","DOG contributed a steady, high amount (15,15) — fits a cautious but reliable contributor: conservative, responsible, willing to support the group without maximizing risk.","GORILLA increased contributions from round 1 to 2 (11→18) and consistently rewarded SLOTH, indicating norm-sensitive behavior and targeted approval; plausible but less consistent with an older conservative who might be more stable in initial generosity."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only contributions and sparse reward actions are observed across two rounds — small sample for inferring personality.","Rewards are anonymous in summaries and only a few reward actions occurred, so motives (strategic vs. altruistic) are ambiguous.","Cultural, ideological, and age-related behavioral tendencies are probabilistic; mapping them to specific game actions involves interpretation and may be imprecise."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"COW","probability":0.6},{"player":"PARROT","probability":0.25},{"player":"MONKEY","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["COW contributed the maximum (20) every round and repeatedly gave positive rewards to many players — matches a stable, consistently pro‑social, charitable profile (churchgoing, patriotic conservatism).","PARROT made steady mid‑to‑high contributions across rounds and actively rewarded others — fits an engaged, reciprocity‑oriented player who cares about group outcomes and social norms.","MONKEY contributed reliably at high levels across rounds (few lows) though sent few rewards — consistent contributor behavior that fits a steady, group‑oriented personality even if less active in reward signaling."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavior in an anonymous lab game may reflect strategic or experimental motives rather than underlying political, religious, or demographic traits.","Rewards and contributions can be driven by short‑term reciprocity, imitation, or noise; similar real‑world personalities could produce different in‑game actions.","Player labels and limited rounds provide only partial observability; matches are probabilistic judgments, not definite identifications."]}
